content.problemStatement: Given an integer xxx, find two non-negative integers aaa and bbb such that (a∧b)+(a∨b)=x(a \wedge b) + (a \vee b) = x(a∧b)+(a∨b)=x, where ∧\wedge∧ is the bitwise AND operation and ∨\vee∨ is the bitwise OR operation.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a single integer x.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print two space-separated non-negative integers a and b such that (a & b) + (a | b) = x.

solution.approach: The key insight here is that for any two non-negative integers a and b, (a & b) + (a | b) = a + b. This is because the bitwise AND operation (&) gives us the bits that are common to both a and b, and the bitwise OR operation (|) gives us the bits that are in either a or b. Therefore, when we add the results of these two operations, we effectively get a + b. Given this, we can simply print 0 and x as our two integers, because (0 & x) + (0 | x) = 0 + x = x.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) per test case, O(T) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — we only use a constant amount of space to store our input and output.

code.Python3:
import sys
data=sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
if not data:
    sys.exit()
T=int(data[0])
out=[]
idx=1
for _ in range(T):
    x=data[idx]
    idx+=1
    out.append(f"0 {x}")
sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))
code.JavaScript: const readline = require('readline'); const rl = readline.createInterface({ input: process.stdin, output: process.stdout }); let T = null; let i = 0; let res = []; rl.on('line', (input) => { if (T === null) { T = parseInt(input); } else { let x = BigInt(input); res.push(`0 ${x}`); i++; if (i === T) { console.log(res.join('\n')); rl.close(); } } });
